info.title: Lendaswap API
info.description: Seamlessly swap between BTC and Stables on Ethereum/Polygon/etc

get.summary: Get the delegate cosigner public key.
get.description:
Clients need this key when preparing delegate PSBTs so the backend
can participate in the batch ceremony as a cosigner.

post.description:
The client prepares and signs delegate PSBTs (intent + forfeits), then
POSTs them here. The backend reconstructs the `Delegate` struct and runs
the full Ark batch ceremony on the client's behalf.

This endpoint blocks until the batch completes (~10-30s).

The client builds an offchain transaction (ark_tx + checkpoints) spending the VHTLC
via the `refund_script` leaf, signs as sender, and sends the partially-signed PSBTs here.
The server cosigns as receiver and returns the countersigned PSBTs.
The client then submits to Arkade for the server (3rd) signature.

403.description: Refund not allowed in current swap state

post.summary: Collaboratively refund recoverable VTXOs via the delegate batch flow.
post.description:
The client prepares delegate PSBTs (intent_proof + forfeits) spending the VHTLC
via the `refund_script` leaf, signs as sender, and sends the partially-signed PSBTs here.
The server cosigns as receiver and returns the countersigned PSBTs.
The client then calls `/api/delegate/settle` to complete the batch ceremony.

403.description: Refund not allowed in current swap state

post.description:
The client builds and partially signs the refund transaction, then POSTs it
here. The server obtains the receiver cosignature and returns the result.
Only allowed when the swap is in `ServerWontFund` or `ClientInvalidFunded`
state.

After receiving the response the client must:
1. Submit the cosigned PSBTs to the Arkade server for the server signature
2. Merge all checkpoint signatures and sign as sender
3. Finalize the transaction with the Arkade server

403.description: Refund not allowed in current swap state

post.summary: Collaboratively refund an EVM HTLC before timelock expiry.
post.description:
The client signs a coordinator-level `CollabRefund` EIP-712 message authorizing
the refund parameters. The server cosigns with an HTLC-level `Refund` signature
(as claimAddress, waiving the timelock) and submits the transaction on-chain.

Supports EVM-to-Arkade, EVM-to-Bitcoin, and EVM-to-Lightning swaps.

403.description: Refund not allowed in current swap state

get.summary: Get EIP-712 parameters for collaborative EVM HTLC refund.
get.description:
Returns the addresses, amounts, and struct fields the client needs to
construct and sign the `CollabRefund` EIP-712 typed data. The client signs
this, then POSTs the signature to the collab-refund-evm endpoint.

Two settlement modes:
- `mode=direct` (default): `sweepToken` = WBTC, `minAmountOut` = 0
- `mode=swap-back`: `sweepToken` = original source token, includes DEX calldata from 1inch

get.summary: Get available EVM tokens from 1inch for supported chains

parameters.name: chain_id
parameters.description: Filter by EVM chain ID (1=Ethereum, 137=Polygon, 42161=Arbitrum). Omit for all chains.

200.description: EVM tokens grouped by chain ID

get.summary: Return the current cached Median Time Past (MTP) and tip height.

200.description: Return the current MTP and tip height

503.description: MTP not available yet (cache not populated)

get.summary: Get a quote for swapping BTC to/from EVM tokens
get.description:
Specify exactly one of source_amount or target_amount.
Amounts are in the smallest unit of the respective token (satoshis for BTC, raw units for EVM
tokens).

parameters.name: source_token
parameters.description: Source token: contract address for EVM tokens, or "btc" for BTC

parameters.name: target_token
parameters.description: Target token: contract address for EVM tokens, or "btc" for BTC

parameters.name: source_amount
parameters.description: Amount in smallest unit of source token (mutually exclusive with target_amount)

parameters.name: target_amount
parameters.description: Amount in smallest unit of target token (mutually exclusive with source_amount)

parameters.name: bridge_target_chain
parameters.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge destination chain (e.g., "Base", "Optimism").
When set, the CCTP forwarding fee is deducted from the target amount.

parameters.name: bridge_source_chain
parameters.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge source chain (e.g., "Optimism", "Base"). Set when
the user's source USDC originates on another CCTP chain and hops to
Arbitrum via CCTPv2 before the HTLC is created. The quote internally
runs the DEX calculation on Arbitrum-native USDC and accounts for the
fast-transfer fee deducted on the burn.

parameters.name: bridge_recipient_setup
parameters.description:
`true` when the recipient's USDC token account on the destination
chain does NOT yet exist and Circle's forwarder needs to create it
(only meaningful for non-EVM destinations like Solana — adds
~$0.05 of ATA rent to `forwardFee`). `false` (default) confirms
the recipient already holds USDC, and is correct for all EVM
destinations. Solana callers are responsible for probing the
destination chain and setting this correctly.

200.description: Return a quote for swapping BTC to/from EVM tokens

get.summary: Probe every external dependency and return their aggregated health.
get.description:
Returns 200 when every service is healthy, 503 otherwise — the body
carries the per-service breakdown either way so callers can distinguish
which dependency is down.

503.description: At least one external dependency is unhealthy

get.summary: Return the configured support agent npubs.
Return all supported swap pairs with their limits (in satoshis) and base fee percentages.

200.description: Supported swap pairs with limits and fees

409.description: Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already

post.description:
Flow:
1. User provides a Lightning invoice they want paid
2. Server creates a Boltz submarine swap
3. User funds the Arkade VHTLC (returned in response)
4. Server claims the Arkade VHTLC and funds the Boltz VHTLC
5. Boltz pays the Lightning invoice

409.description: Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already

post.summary: Create a new Bitcoin (on-chain) to Arkade swap request
post.description:
The user provides an on-chain BTC amount to swap and receives Arkade VTXOs.
Flow:
1. User sends BTC to the Taproot HTLC address returned
2. After 1 confirmation, server creates Arkade VHTLC for user
3. User claims Arkade VHTLC with secret, revealing it
4. Server claims on-chain BTC with the revealed secret

409.description: Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already
Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already
Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already
Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already

post.description:
Flow:
1. User provides their Lightning invoice (they want to receive BTC)
2. Server prepares Boltz submarine swap to pay the invoice
3. User funds EVM HTLCErc20 with tokens
4. Server pays Lightning invoice via Boltz (gets preimage)
5. Server claims EVM HTLCErc20 using preimage

409.description: Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already

post.description:
Flow:
1. User pays the Lightning invoice returned
2. Server receives BTC via Boltz and creates Arkade VHTLC for user
3. User claims Arkade VHTLC with secret, revealing it
4. Server claims Boltz VHTLC with the revealed secret

409.description: Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already
Conflict - a swap with this preimage hash exists already

post.summary: Recover swaps by deriving user_id keys from an Xpub
post.description:
This endpoint allows clients to recover their swap history after importing a mnemonic. The
client provides an Xpub they used to derive `user_id`s, and the server derives `user_id` keys at
m/9419/121923/<index> for indices 0-99 and searches for matching swaps.

get.summary: Get swap status and details by ID

post.summary: Claims an EVM-targeted swap gaslessly via the HTLCCoordinator contract.
post.description:
Supports Arkade-to-EVM, Lightning and Bitcoin-to-EVM swaps. The server submits
`coordinator.redeemAndExecute` on the client's behalf, using the client's
EIP-712 signature and secret.

# Prerequisites — what the client must do before calling this endpoint

1. **Create a swap** via one of the swap endpoints (e.g. `POST /swap/arkade/evm`). Wait for the
   swap status to reach `ServerFunded`.

2. **Fetch calldata** via [`GET
   /swap/{id}/redeem-and-swap-calldata`](#/redeem-and-swap-calldata/get_dex_calldata). This
   returns:
   - `dex_calldata` — DEX swap calldata (only for non-WBTC targets)
   - `gasless_fee_sats` — the fee deducted from the HTLC amount
   - `calls_hash` — `keccak256(abi.encode(calls))`, must be included in the signature

3. **Construct the EIP-712 signature** over the `Redeem` struct:

   **Domain:**
   ```text
   EIP712Domain(string name, string version, uint256 chainId, address verifyingContract)
   name            = "HTLCErc20"
   version         = "3"
   chainId         = <target EVM chain ID>
   verifyingContract = <HTLCErc20 contract address (swap.evm_htlc_address)>
   ```

   **Struct:**
   ```text
   Redeem(
       bytes32 preimage,       // the swap secret
       uint256 amount,         // WBTC amount in the HTLC (in sats)
       address token,          // WBTC token address
       address sender,         // server's EVM address
       uint256 timelock,       // HTLC refund locktime
       address caller,         // HTLCCoordinator contract address
       address destination,    // where output tokens go
       address sweepToken,     // target token (or WBTC if direct)
       uint256 minAmountOut,   // slippage protection (0 for now)
       bytes32 callsHash       // from step 2
   )
   ```

   The final digest is: `keccak256(\x19\x01 || domainSeparator || structHash)`

   Sign this digest with the client's EVM private key (secp256k1) to obtain
   `v`, `r`, `s`.

   **JavaScript example (using ethers v6):**

   ```javascript
   import { ethers } from "ethers";

   // 1. Fetch calldata (returns calls_hash + optional dex_calldata)
   const { calls_hash, dex_calldata, gasless_fee_sats } = await fetch(
     `${baseUrl}/swap/${swapId}/redeem-and-swap-calldata?destination=${destination}`
   ).then(r => r.json());

   // 2. Determine sweepToken
   const needsDexSwap = targetTokenAddress.toLowerCase() !== wbtcAddress.toLowerCase();
   const sweepToken = needsDexSwap ? targetTokenAddress : wbtcAddress;

   // 3. Build EIP-712 typed data and sign
   const wallet = new ethers.Wallet(privateKey);

   const domain = {
     name: "HTLCErc20",
     version: "3",
     chainId: swap.evm_chain_id,               // e.g. 42161 for Arbitrum
     verifyingContract: swap.evm_htlc_address,  // HTLCErc20 contract
   };

   const types = {
     Redeem: [
       { name: "preimage",     type: "bytes32" },
       { name: "amount",       type: "uint256" },
       { name: "token",        type: "address" },
       { name: "sender",       type: "address" },
       { name: "timelock",     type: "uint256" },
       { name: "caller",       type: "address" },
       { name: "destination",  type: "address" },
       { name: "sweepToken",   type: "address" },
       { name: "minAmountOut", type: "uint256" },
       { name: "callsHash",   type: "bytes32" },
     ],
   };

   const message = {
     preimage:     secret,                        // 0x-prefixed 32-byte hex
     amount:       swap.evm_expected_sats,        // WBTC sats locked in HTLC
     token:        wbtcAddress,                   // WBTC on the target chain
     sender:       swap.server_evm_address,       // server (HTLC creator)
     timelock:     swap.evm_refund_locktime,      // HTLC expiry timestamp
     caller:       swap.evm_coordinator_address,  // HTLCCoordinator contract
     destination:  destination,                   // where output tokens go
     sweepToken:   sweepToken,
     minAmountOut: 0,                             // no slippage check for now
     callsHash:    calls_hash,                    // from step 1
   };

   const sig = await wallet.signTypedData(domain, types, message);
   const { v, r, s } = ethers.Signature.from(sig);

   // 4. POST to claim-gasless
   await fetch(`${baseUrl}/swap/${swapId}/claim-gasless`, {
     method: "POST",
     headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
     body: JSON.stringify({
       secret, destination, v, r, s,
       dex_calldata: needsDexSwap ? dex_calldata : undefined,
     }),
   });
   ```

4. **POST** the request to this endpoint with the secret, destination, signature, and optionally
   the `dex_calldata` from step 2.

200.description: Swap claimed successfully via gasless execution

400.description: Bad request - swap not found or in wrong state

post.summary: Fund an EVM-sourced swap gaslessly via Permit2.
post.description:
The server submits the `executeAndCreateWithPermit2` transaction on the
depositor's behalf, pulling tokens via the depositor's Permit2 signature.
If an EIP-2612 permit is provided, the server first submits that to approve
the Permit2 contract.

200.description: Swap funded successfully via gasless relay

400.description: Bad request - swap not found or in wrong state

get.summary: Returns the gasless claim data for an EVM-targeted swap.
get.description:
For non-WBTC targets: fetches fresh 1inch DEX calldata with `receiver = destination`.
For WBTC-direct targets: returns only the gasless fee and calls_hash (no DEX data).

Always returns `calls_hash` which the client must include in the EIP-712 redeem signature.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: EVM destination address for output tokens

200.description: Fresh DEX calldata with gasless fee estimate

get.summary: Returns calldata to refund an expired EVM-to-Arkade HTLC via the coordinator.
get.description:
Two modes:
- `mode=swap-back` (default): calls `refundAndExecute` — refund WBTC from expired HTLC, swap
  WBTC → source token via 1inch, sweep to caller.
- `mode=direct`: calls `refundTo` — refund WBTC directly to caller (no DEX).

Both are permissionless after the timelock expires.

get.summary: Returns structured data for funding an EVM-to-BTC swap via Permit2.
get.description:
Instead of pre-encoded calldata, this returns the individual components
so the SDK can construct the Permit2 EIP-712 signature and
`executeAndCreateWithPermit2` calldata client-side.

The calls array contains only [approve(dexRouter, amount), dexSwap(...)].
No `transferFrom` — Permit2 handles the token pull.

get.summary:
Returns data for funding an EVM-to-BTC swap via a user's 4337 smart
account. The client composes a UserOperation that executes receiveMessage
(for CCTP-inbound) + USDC.approve(Permit2, max) + executeAndCreateWithPermit2
atomically — `destinationCaller` on the source-chain burn is the smart
account address, so CCTPv2's receiveMessage gating is pinned to it.
Get list of available tokens for swaps.
get.description:
Optionally pass `source_chain` + `source_token` **or** `target_chain` + `target_token`
to filter the response to valid counterparty tokens.

parameters.name: source_chain
parameters.description: If set together with `source_token`, return only valid target tokens.

parameters.name: target_chain
parameters.description: If set together with `target_token`, return only valid source tokens.

200.description: Return list of available tokens for swaps

get.summary: Return the current git tag and commit hash.

200.description: Return the deployed version and commit hash

AaConfig.description:
ERC-4337 / Kernel specific addresses the client uses to derive its
smart-account sender + compose `initCode`.

account_factory.description: Kernel (ZeroDev) factory address on this chain.

salt.description:
Salt used when deriving the smart-account address. Pinned to "0"
so each user has exactly one smart account per signing key.

ArkadeToEvmSwapRequest.description:
Chain-agnostic request for Arkade-to-EVM swaps.

The caller specifies the target chain via `evm_chain_id` and the token
via its ERC-20 contract `token_address`. This endpoint supports any token
reachable through 1inch aggregation (which covers Uniswap, Curve, etc.).

amount_in.description:
How many sats the user wants to send (mutually exclusive with `amount_out`).
Value is in satoshis (smallest BTC unit).

amount_out.description:
How much target token the user wants to receive (mutually exclusive with `amount_in`).
Value is in the target token's smallest unit (e.g. for USDC with 6 decimals, 1000000 = 1
USDC).

bridge_recipient_setup.description:
ATA-existence flag for non-EVM CCTP destinations (Solana).
See `BitcoinToEvmSwapRequest::bridge_recipient_setup`.

bridge_target_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge destination chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Arbitrum"). When set,
USDC will be bridged to this chain after the DEX swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

claiming_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim (SDK-derived for gasless claims).

gasless.description:
Whether the server should execute the DEX swap on behalf of the user (gasless claim).
When true, the gasless network fee is added to the total fee charged.
Defaults to true.

hash_lock.description: Hash lock provided by the client (32-byte hex string with 0x prefix).

refund_pk.description: Refund public key used to generate the Arkade VHTLC.

target_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim (user's final destination).

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the desired token on the target chain.

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

bridge_target_chain.description: CCTP bridge destination chain. When set, USDC is bridged cross-chain after the swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

client_evm_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim (SDK-derived for gasless claims).

target_evm_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim (user's final destination).

wbtc_address.description: WBTC token contract address on the target EVM chain (the token locked in the HTLC).

ArkadeToLightningCollabRefundRequest.description: Request for a collaborative VHTLC refund.

ark_tx.description: Base64-encoded ark transaction PSBT, partially signed by the sender (client).

ArkadeToLightningCollabRefundResponse.description: Response with cosigned PSBTs for a collaborative refund.

ark_tx.description: Base64-encoded ark transaction PSBT with receiver cosignature added.

checkpoint.description: Base64-encoded checkpoint PSBT with receiver cosignature added.

ArkadeToLightningSwapRequest.description:
Request for creating an Arkade-to-Lightning swap.

The user sends Arkade VTXOs and a Lightning invoice gets paid.

amount_sats.description:
Amount in satoshis the recipient should receive on Lightning.
Required when `lightning_address` or `lnurl` is provided; ignored when
`lightning_invoice` is provided (amount is read from the invoice).

lightning_address.description:
Lightning address (e.g. `user@speed.app`) to resolve via LNURL-pay.
Mutually exclusive with `lightning_invoice` and `lnurl`. Requires `amount_sats`.

lightning_invoice.description: Lightning BOLT11 invoice the user wants paid.

lnurl.description:
Raw LNURL string (e.g. `lnurl1...`) to resolve via LNURL-pay.
Mutually exclusive with `lightning_invoice` and `lightning_address`. Requires
`amount_sats`.

referral_code.description: Optional referral code for fee tracking.

refund_pk.description: User's refund public key for the Arkade VHTLC (if swap fails/expires).

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

arkade_claim_txid.description: Arkade VHTLC claim transaction ID (server claim)

boltz_amount_sats.description: Amount server must fund on Boltz VHTLC

boltz_vhtlc_address.description: Boltz VHTLC address (server funds for Boltz to claim)

receiver_pk.description: Server's claim public key (receiver in the VHTLC)

sender_pk.description: User's refund public key (sender in the VHTLC)

source_amount.description: Amount user sends on Arkade in sats

target_amount.description: Amount user receives via Lightning in sats

unilateral_refund_without_receiver_delay.description: Unilateral refund without receiver delay in seconds

claim_pk.description: User's claim public key for the Arkade VHTLC

hash_lock.description:
Hash lock provided by the client (20-byte hex string, no 0x prefix).
The client generates a secret and computes HASH160(secret) = RIPEMD160(SHA256(secret)).
This hash is used for both the on-chain Bitcoin HTLC (OP_HASH160) and the Arkade VHTLC.
Client will use the secret to claim the Arkade VHTLC later.

referral_code.description: Optional referral code for fee exemption

refund_pk.description: User's refund public key for the on-chain Bitcoin HTLC

sats_receive.description: Amount the user wants to receive on Arkade in satoshis

target_arkade_address.description: User's target Arkade address to receive VTXOs

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes

BitcoinToEvmSwapRequest.description:
Chain-agnostic request for Arkade-to-EVM swaps.

The caller specifies the target chain via `evm_chain_id` and the token
via its ERC-20 contract `token_address`. This endpoint supports any token
reachable through 1inch aggregation (which covers Uniswap, Curve, etc.).

amount_in.description:
How many sats the user wants to send (mutually exclusive with `amount_out`).
Value is in satoshis (smallest BTC unit).

amount_out.description:
How much target token the user wants to receive (mutually exclusive with `amount_in`).
Value is in the target token's smallest unit (e.g. for USDC with 6 decimals, 1000000 = 1
USDC).

bridge_recipient_setup.description:
ATA-existence flag for non-EVM CCTP destinations (Solana).
`true` = recipient has no USDC ATA yet (Circle's forwarder
creates one, paid out of the burn). `false` (default) = recipient
already holds USDC, or destination is EVM. Drives the bridge-fee
variant used when inflating the DEX target and must match the
value later passed to `redeem-and-swap-calldata` so the burn
doesn't underflow. Solana callers are responsible for probing
the destination chain and setting this correctly.

bridge_target_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge destination chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Arbitrum"). When set,
USDC will be bridged to this chain after the DEX swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

claiming_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim (SDK-derived for gasless claims).

gasless.description:
Whether the server should execute the DEX swap on behalf of the user (gasless claim).
When true, the gasless network fee is added to the total fee charged.
Defaults to true.

hash_lock.description: Hash lock provided by the client (32-byte hex string with 0x prefix).

refund_pk.description: Refund public key used to generate the Arkade VHTLC.

target_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim (user's final destination).

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the desired token on the target chain.

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

bridge_target_chain.description: CCTP bridge destination chain. When set, USDC is bridged cross-chain after the swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

btc_hash_lock.description:
Hash lock (20-byte hex)

To be used for bitcoin htlc. This is the [`ripemd160(evm_hash_lock)`]

btc_htlc_address.description: On-chain Bitcoin HTLC P2WSH address (user sends BTC here)

btc_server_pk.description: The server's pk inside the htlc

client_evm_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim (SDK-derived for gasless claims)

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the swap was created

evm_claim_txid.description: EVM HTLC claim transaction ID (user claim)

evm_expected_sats.description: Amount of WBTC sats locked in the EVM HTLC

evm_hash_lock.description:
Hash lock (32-byte hex)

To be used for EVM htlc

source_amount.description: Amount user must send in satoshis

target_amount.description: How much the user will receive in the target token's smallest unit

target_evm_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim (user's final destination)

wbtc_address.description: WBTC token contract address on the target EVM chain (the token locked in the HTLC)

arkade_claim_txid.description: Arkade VHTLC claim transaction ID (user claim)

asset_amount.description:
Amount user must send in satoshis
Deprecated: please use [`target_amount`]

btc_htlc_address.description: On-chain Bitcoin HTLC P2WSH address (user sends BTC here)

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the swap was created

sats_receive.description: Amount user will receive on Arkade in satoshis

server_vhtlc_pk.description: Server's VHTLC public key (sender in the VHTLC)

source_amount.description: Amount user must send in satoshis

unilateral_refund_without_receiver_delay.description: Unilateral refund without receiver delay in seconds

CallJson.description: A single call in the coordinator's Call[] array, serialized as JSON.

Chain.description:
Supported blockchain networks.
EVM chains serialize to their chain ID as a string (e.g. "137" for Polygon).

ClaimGaslessRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /swap/{id}/claim-gasless`.

The client provides the HTLC preimage (secret) and an EIP-712 signature
authorising the server to call `redeemAndExecute` on the coordinator
contract on the client's behalf.

# Examples

Minimal request (WBTC-direct target, no DEX swap):

```
let request = serde_json::json!({
    "secret": "0x0101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101",
    "destination": "0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045",
    "v": 27,
    "r": "0xaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa",
    "s": "0xbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb"
});
```

Request with DEX calldata (non-WBTC target, e.g. USDC):

```
let request = serde_json::json!({
    "secret": "0x0101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101010101",
    "destination": "0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045",
    "v": 28,
    "r": "0xaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa",
    "s": "0xbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb",
    "dex_calldata": {
        "to": "0x1111111254EEB25477B68fb85Ed929f73A960582",
        "data": "0xabcdef0123456789",
        "value": "0"
    }
});
```

bridge_recipient.description:
Optional non-EVM bridge recipient (e.g. a Solana base58 SPL pubkey).
Used as CCTP `mintRecipient` when `bridge_target_chain` is non-EVM;
must match the value passed to `redeem-and-swap-calldata` so the
rebuilt `calls_hash` matches the EIP-712 signature.

For Solana: this is the recipient's USDC ATA, not their wallet.

bridge_recipient_wallet.description:
Optional Solana wallet pubkey, supplied alongside `bridge_recipient`
when the recipient's USDC ATA does not yet exist. Triggers the
extended 65-byte forwarding hookData. Must mirror what was passed to
`redeem-and-swap-calldata` so the rebuilt `calls_hash` matches.

destination.description: EVM address where tokens should be sent

oneOf.description:
Optional DEX calldata from the client. If provided, the server uses this
instead of fetching from 1inch. Client should fetch via
`GET /swap/{id}/redeem-and-swap-calldata?destination=...`

r.description: EIP-712 signature r (32-byte hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

s.description: EIP-712 signature s (32-byte hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

secret.description: The secret/preimage (32-byte hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

CollabRefundDelegateRequest.description: Request for collaborative refund of recoverable VTXOs (delegate batch flow).

forfeit_psbts.description: Base64-encoded forfeit PSBTs (partially signed by the sender/client).

intent_proof.description: Base64-encoded intent proof PSBT (partially signed by the sender/client).

CollabRefundDexCalldata.description: DEX calldata returned by the params endpoint for swap-back mode.

CollabRefundEvmParams.description: EIP-712 parameters the client needs to sign for collaborative refund.

amount.description: WBTC amount locked in the HTLC (decimal string)

calls_hash.description: keccak256(abi.encode(calls)) for the exact calls array the depositor signs over.

oneOf.description: DEX calldata for the swap-back. Only present when mode=swap-back.

min_amount_out.description:
Minimum output amount for the sweep (0 for direct, DEX-quoted for swap-back).
This is the `minAmountOut` field in the EIP-712 struct.

mode.description: Settlement mode used to compute sweep fields.

server_signer_address.description: Server's signer EOA address (the `caller` field in the EIP-712 struct)

source_token_address.description:
Source token address (the token the user originally deposited).
Only relevant for swap-back mode.

sweep_token.description:
Token the depositor receives after refund (WBTC for direct, source token for swap-back).
This is the `sweepToken` field in the EIP-712 struct.

CollabRefundEvmRequest.description: Request for collaborative refund of an EVM HTLC.

depositor_address.description:
On-chain depositor address (the address that funded the HTLC via the coordinator).
In the Permit2 flow this is a derived key, not the user's wallet address.
Required so the server can sign the correct HTLC refund digest.

min_amount_out.description: Minimum amount out for the sweep (0 = no minimum). Defaults to 0.

mode.description:
Refund mode: "direct" (WBTC back to depositor) or "swap"/"swap-back" (DEX swap-back to
original token)

r.description: EIP-712 signature r (32-byte hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

s.description: EIP-712 signature s (32-byte hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

sweep_token.description:
Token to sweep to depositor after refund. Required for "swap" mode.
For "direct" mode, defaults to WBTC.

CollabRefundEvmResponse.description: Response for collaborative refund of an EVM HTLC.

tx_hash.description: Transaction hash of the on-chain refund

CollabRefundRequest.description: Request for collaborative refund of spendable VTXOs (offchain send flow).

ark_tx.description: Base64-encoded ark transaction PSBT (partially signed by the sender/client).

cosigner_pk.description: Hex-encoded compressed public key of the delegate cosigner.

client_pk.description: Client's public key for the VHTLC

vtxos.description: List of VTXO outpoints to refresh

DexCallData.description: DEX swap calldata for the coordinator contract.

DexCalldata.description: DEX calldata for the swap (from 1inch or another aggregator)

data.description: Encoded swap calldata (hex string, with or without 0x prefix)

Eip2612Permit.description: EIP-2612 permit signature for gasless token->Permit2 approval.

vtxos.description: List of VTXO outpoints to refresh ("txid:vout" format)

expected_vtxo_expiry.description:
Expected expiry timestamp (Unix) of the resulting VTXOs.
This is the minimum expiry among the server's VTXOs that will be used to fund the swap.

EvmToArkadeGenericSwapRequest.description:
Request to create an EVM-to-Arkade swap.

User sends any ERC-20 token on EVM, receives BTC on Arkade.

amount_in.description: Source token amount in smallest units (mutually exclusive with `amount_out`).

amount_out.description: Desired BTC output in sats (mutually exclusive with `amount_in`).

bridge_source_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge source chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Optimism"). When set,
the user's source USDC originates on this chain and hops through CCTPv2 to
Arbitrum before the HTLC is created. The backend pads `deposit_amount` by
the fast-transfer fee at UserOp-calldata time.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge source chain.

gasless.description: Whether to use gasless relay for funding (server submits tx on behalf of user).

hash_lock.description: Hash lock (0x-prefixed 32-byte hex). Client generates secret, computes SHA256(secret).

target_address.description: Arkade address where the user wants to receive BTC.

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the source token on the EVM chain.

user_address.description: User's EVM address (sender of the ERC-20 token).

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

bridge_source_chain.description:
CCTP bridge source chain. Set when the source USDC originated on
another CCTP chain and hopped to Arbitrum via CCTPv2 before the
HTLC was created. `source_token` still reports the post-hop
Arbitrum USDC; this field tells the SDK what chain the user is
expected to burn from.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Native USDC address on the bridge source chain.

gasless.description: Whether this swap was created with gasless relay (Permit2)

EvmToBitcoinSwapRequest.description:
Request to create an EVM-to-Bitcoin swap.

User sends any ERC-20 token on EVM, receives on-chain BTC (Taproot HTLC).
The caller specifies the source chain via `evm_chain_id` and the token
via its ERC-20 contract `token_address`.

amount_in.description: Source token amount in smallest units (mutually exclusive with `amount_out`).

amount_out.description: Desired BTC output in sats (mutually exclusive with `amount_in`).

bridge_source_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge source chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Optimism"). When set,
the user's source USDC originates on this chain and hops through CCTPv2 to
Arbitrum before the HTLC is created. The backend pads `deposit_amount` by
the fast-transfer fee at UserOp-calldata time.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge source chain.

claim_pk.description: User's BTC public key for claiming BTC from the on-chain Taproot HTLC.

gasless.description: Whether to use gasless relay for funding (server submits tx on behalf of user).

hash_lock.description: Hash lock (0x-prefixed 32-byte hex). Client generates secret, computes SHA256(secret).

target_address.description: User's target BTC address to receive claimed funds.

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the source token on the EVM chain.

user_address.description: User's EVM address (sender of the ERC-20 token).

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

bridge_source_chain.description:
CCTP bridge source chain. Set when the source USDC originated on
another CCTP chain and hopped to Arbitrum via CCTPv2 before the
HTLC was created. `source_token` still reports the post-hop
Arbitrum USDC; this field tells the SDK what chain the user is
expected to burn from.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Native USDC address on the bridge source chain.

btc_hash_lock.description: BTC hash lock (RIPEMD160 of EVM hash lock)

btc_htlc_address.description: On-chain BTC Taproot HTLC address (server creates, user claims)

btc_server_refund_pk.description: Server's x-only public key for refunding BTC

btc_user_claim_pk.description: User's x-only public key for claiming BTC

client_evm_address.description: User's EVM address (funds the EVM HTLC)

evm_expected_sats.description: Expected WBTC amount (in sats) on EVM

evm_htlc_address.description: EVM HTLC contract address (user creates, server claims)

gasless.description: Whether this swap was created with gasless relay (Permit2)

server_evm_address.description: Server's EVM address (claims the EVM HTLC)

source_amount.description: Source token amount in smallest units

target_amount.description: Expected BTC amount (in sats) user will receive

target_btc_address.description: User's target BTC address for receiving claimed funds

wbtc_address.description: WBTC token contract address on the EVM chain

EvmToLightningSwapRequest.description:
Request to create an EVM-to-Lightning swap.

User sends any ERC-20 token on EVM, receives BTC via Lightning.
The hash_lock is derived from the Lightning invoice's payment hash.

Provide **one of**:
- `lightning_invoice` — a BOLT11 invoice
- `lightning_address` + `amount_sats` — a Lightning address resolved via LNURL-pay
- `lnurl` + `amount_sats` — a raw LNURL string (bech32-encoded URL) resolved via LNURL-pay

amount_sats.description:
Amount in satoshis the recipient should receive on Lightning.
Required when `lightning_address` or `lnurl` is provided; ignored when
`lightning_invoice` is provided (amount is read from the invoice).

bridge_source_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge source chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Optimism"). When set,
the user's source USDC originates on this chain and hops through CCTPv2 to
Arbitrum before the HTLC is created. The backend pads `deposit_amount` by
the fast-transfer fee at UserOp-calldata time.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge source chain.

gasless.description: Whether to use gasless relay for funding (server submits tx on behalf of user).

lightning_address.description:
Lightning address (e.g. `user@speed.app`) to resolve via LNURL-pay.
Mutually exclusive with `lightning_invoice` and `lnurl`. Requires `amount_sats`.

lightning_invoice.description:
User's Lightning BOLT11 invoice to receive payment.
Mutually exclusive with `lightning_address` and `lnurl`.

lnurl.description:
Raw LNURL string (e.g. `lnurl1...`) to resolve via LNURL-pay.
Mutually exclusive with `lightning_invoice` and `lightning_address`. Requires
`amount_sats`.

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the source token on the EVM chain.

user_address.description: User's EVM address (sender of the ERC-20 token).

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

bridge_source_chain.description:
CCTP bridge source chain. Set when the source USDC originated on
another CCTP chain and hopped to Arbitrum via CCTPv2 before the
HTLC was created. `source_token` still reports the post-hop
Arbitrum USDC; this field tells the SDK what chain the user is
expected to burn from.

bridge_source_token_address.description: Native USDC address on the bridge source chain.

client_lightning_invoice.description: User's Lightning invoice to receive payment

gasless.description: Whether this swap was created with gasless relay (Permit2)

lightning_paid.description: Whether the Lightning payment has been made

calls.description: The calls array (includes fee transfer + DEX swap)

oneOf.description: Optional EIP-2612 permit for gasless token->Permit2 approval

permit2_signature.description: Compact Permit2 signature (hex: r || s || v)

LightningToArkadeSwapRequest.description:
Request for creating a Lightning-to-Arkade swap.

The user pays a Lightning invoice and receives Arkade VTXOs.

claim_pk.description: User's claim public key for the Arkade VHTLC

hash_lock.description:
Hash lock provided by the client (32-byte hex string with 0x prefix).
Used for the Boltz VHTLC. RIPEMD160 of this is used for the Arkade VHTLC.

referral_code.description: Optional referral code for fee exemption

sats_receive.description: Amount the user wants to receive on Arkade in satoshis

target_arkade_address.description: User's target Arkade address to receive VTXOs

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes

arkade_claim_txid.description: Arkade VHTLC claim transaction ID (user claim)

arkade_vhtlc_address.description: Arkade VHTLC address (server creates, user claims)

boltz_invoice.description:
Lightning invoice to pay
DEPRECATED use [`boltz_invoice`] instead

btc_claim_txid.description: Server's VHTLC claim transaction ID (Boltz claim)

receiver_pk.description: User's claim public key (receiver in the VHTLC)

sender_pk.description: Server's VHTLC public key (sender in the VHTLC)

source_amount.description: Amount user must send via Lightning in sats

target_amount.description: Amount user will receive on Arkade in sats

unilateral_refund_without_receiver_delay.description: Unilateral refund without receiver delay in seconds

LightningToEvmSwapRequest.description:
Chain-agnostic request for Lightning-to-EVM swaps.

The caller specifies the target chain via `evm_chain_id` and the token
via its ERC-20 contract `token_address`. This endpoint supports any token
reachable through 1inch aggregation.

amount_in.description:
How many sats the user wants to send (mutually exclusive with `amount_out`).
Value is in satoshis (smallest BTC unit).

amount_out.description:
How much target token the user wants to receive (mutually exclusive with `amount_in`).
Value is in the target token's smallest unit (e.g. for USDC with 6 decimals, 1000000 = 1
USDC).

bridge_recipient_setup.description:
ATA-existence flag for non-EVM CCTP destinations (Solana).
See `BitcoinToEvmSwapRequest::bridge_recipient_setup`.

bridge_target_chain.description:
Optional: CCTP bridge destination chain (e.g., "Ethereum", "Arbitrum"). When set,
USDC will be bridged to this chain after the DEX swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: Optional: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

claiming_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim (SDK-derived for gasless claims).

gasless.description:
Whether the server should execute the DEX swap on behalf of the user (gasless claim).
When true, the gasless network fee is added to the total fee charged.
Defaults to true.

hash_lock.description: Hash lock provided by the client (32-byte hex string with 0x prefix).

refund_pk.description: Refund public key used to generate the Arkade VHTLC.

target_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim (user's final destination).

token_address.description: ERC-20 contract address of the desired token on the target chain.

user_id.description: User ID derived from wallet for recovery purposes.

boltz_invoice.description:
Lightning invoice to pay
DEPRECATED: use [`bolt11_invoice`] instead

bridge_target_chain.description: CCTP bridge destination chain. When set, USDC is bridged cross-chain after the swap.

bridge_target_token_address.description: USDC address on the bridge destination chain.

btc_claim_txid.description: Server's claim transaction ID on Arkade (Boltz VHTLC claim)

client_evm_address.description: EVM address that will sign the HTLC claim

target_evm_address.description: EVM address where tokens are swept after the claim

wbtc_address.description: WBTC token contract address on the target EVM chain

mtp.description: The Median Time Past (Unix timestamp in seconds).

tip_height.description: The tip block height when MTP was last calculated.

bridge_fee.description:
CCTP bridge forwarding fee in USDC smallest units (6 decimals).
Only present when bridge_target_chain was specified in the quote request.

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate: how much of the EVM token you get/pay per BTC

gasless_network_fee.description:
Gasless network fee estimate (in satoshis) — covers the additional gas
the server pays to execute the DEX swap on behalf of the user
(redeemAndExecute via the coordinator contract).

max_amount.description: Maximum BTC value of the swap in satoshis

min_amount.description: Minimum BTC value of the swap in satoshis

network_fee.description:
Network fee estimate (in satoshis) — covers server-paid gas for HTLC
create/claim + BTC mining fee when applicable.

source_amount.description: Pre-calculated source amount in smallest unit of source token (pre-fee).

target_amount.description: Pre-calculated target amount in smallest unit of target token (pre-fee).

index.description: The derivation index used for this swap

xpub.description:
Extended public key derived from the user's wallet.

Used to derive `user_id`s for that user.

highest_index.description:
The highest index used in any recovered swap.

Client should set their next derivation index to this + 1.

swaps.description: List of recovered swaps with their derivation indices

RedeemAndSwapResponse.description: Response containing fresh DEX calldata and the gasless fee that will be deducted at claim time.

calls_hash.description:
keccak256(abi.encode(calls)) — the hash of the full calls array (including fee transfer).
Must be included in the client's EIP-712 redeem signature to prevent call substitution.

oneOf.description: DEX swap calldata from 1inch. None for direct WBTC swaps (no DEX component).

gasless_fee_sats.description:
Gasless fee in WBTC sats, estimated at calldata-fetch time.
The claim endpoint will deduct this from the HTLC amount via a WBTC transfer.

RefundCalldataResponse.description: Response containing refund calldata for an EVM-to-Arkade swap.

calldata.description: ABI-encoded calldata for the refund call on the coordinator

ServiceStatus.description:
Per-service health datapoint.

Failures intentionally do not include the underlying error: transport
errors from RPC clients can echo back full URLs (with embedded API keys)
and other internal topology. Detailed errors are logged server-side; the
public response only carries a sanitized `"unavailable"` marker.

block_height.description: Tip / block height for chain-backed services (Bitcoin and EVMs).

block_time.description: Tip block timestamp (Unix seconds) for chain-backed services.

cosigner_pk.description: Hex-encoded compressed cosigner public key (must match our static key).

forfeit_psbts.description: List of base64-encoded partial forfeit PSBTs (signed with SIGHASH_ALL|ANYONECANPAY).

intent_proof.description: Base64-encoded intent proof PSBT (signed by the VTXO owner).

preimage.description: Optional hex-encoded preimage (the HTLC secret). Required together with `swap_id`.

swap_id.description:
Optional swap ID. When provided alongside `preimage`, the backend marks the
swap as ClientRedeemed so the EVM claimer can proceed.

commitment_txid.description: Commitment transaction ID from the batch settlement.

StatusResponse.description: Aggregated health of every external dependency.

healthy.description: True if every probed service is healthy.

SwapPairInfo.description: A supported swap pair with its limits and base fee.

fee_percentage.description:
Fee percentage as a decimal (e.g. 0.0025 = 0.25%).

For a swap of 100,000 sats at 0.25% fee, the fee would be 250 sats.
Swaps involving Lightning may incur additional Lightning Network routing fees
on top of this percentage.

SwapPairsResponse.description: All supported swap pairs with their limits and fees.

SwapStatus.description:
Atomic swap state machine for BTC --> Target Asset swaps using HTLCs.

# Overview

This enum tracks the state of an atomic swap between Bitcoin (via Lightning/Arkade)
and Target Asset (on Polygon, Ethereum, etc). The swap uses Hash Time-Locked Contracts (HTLCs)
to ensure atomicity without requiring trust.

# Normal Flow

```text
Pending → ClientFunded → ServerFunded → ClientRedeemed → ServerRedeemed
```

1. Client creates swap request → `Pending`
2. Client sends BTC → `ClientFunded`
3. Server locks WBTC in Polygon HTLC → `ServerFunded`
4. Client reveals secret to claim Target Asset → `ClientRedeemed`
5. Server uses secret to claim BTC → `ServerRedeemed` (terminal)

# Refund Flows

```text
Pending → Expired (no funding)
ClientFunded → ClientRefunded (client refunds before server funds)
ServerFunded → ClientFundedServerRefunded (HTLC timeout)
```

# State Diagram

See `swap_states.md` for a complete state diagram and detailed documentation.

# Security

- Bitcoin HTLC timeout must be longer than Polygon HTLC timeout
- Secret becomes public when client claims TargetAsset on Polygon
- `ClientRefundedServerFunded` is an error state that should never occur

UseropFundingCalldataResponse.description:
Response: same HTLC + DEX payload the Permit2 endpoint returns, plus
an `aa` object with the addresses a 4337 client needs.

VtxoSwapStatus.description:
VTXO Swap state machine for BTC-to-BTC (Arkade refresh) swaps.

# Overview

This enum tracks the state of a VTXO refresh swap where users exchange
old VTXOs for new ones with refreshed expiry times.

# Normal Flow

```text
Pending -> ClientFunded -> ServerFunded -> ClientRedeemed -> ServerRedeemed
```

1. Swap created -> `Pending`
2. Client funds their VHTLC -> `ClientFunded`
3. Server funds their VHTLC -> `ServerFunded`
4. Client claims server's VHTLC (reveals preimage) -> `ClientRedeemed`
5. Server claims client's VHTLC -> `ServerRedeemed` (terminal)

# Refund Flows

```text
Pending -> Expired (no funding)
ClientFunded -> ClientRefunded (server didn't fund in time)
ServerFunded -> ClientFundedServerRefunded (client didn't claim in time)
```
